title: Why is May 1 a paid public holiday in France? The history of a hard-won celebration
subhead: The day celebrating worker and union movements is now celebrated in most countries around the world. In France, exceptions to the work ban on this day remain a subject of ongoing debate.

title: In Brazil's 'slaughterhouse capital,' workers are under pressure: 'The companies want us to be robots'
EU-Mercosur trade deal enters into force: Five questions about where things stand
French authors face a reckoning
subhead: Nearly 200 writers have expressed their desire to leave the Grasset publishing house since the dismissal of its chief, Olivier Nora. This mass departure has highlighted their dependence on the publisher, especially regarding rights.

title: 'Writing helps us organize our ideas. How will we understand the world if we let AI do the writing?'
subhead: Le Monde columnist Philippe Bernard explores the questions raised by the decline of writing and the rise of artificial intelligence for our cognitive abilities.

title: Fally Ipupa, king of 2.0 rumba, at the Stade de France
subhead: The Congolese singer prepares to celebrate his 20-year career and the evolution of his rumba, from his first album, 'Droit chemin,' to his latest, 'XX,' released on April 17.

title: Seafood and veggie tide inundates dining tables on the Basque coast
subhead: Young talents focused on vegetables and fish have elevated the gastronomic offerings in Biarritz and its neighboring town Bidart, in a region traditionally passionate about meat.

In a small French village, a father is charged with sexual abuse of 34 victims aged 3 to 9
subhead: A tree, a rope, a letter. After surviving a suicide attempt, a man revealed he had committed numerous crimes against children. After a year-long and controversial investigation, he was charged, on April 10, with rape and sexual assault of minors and recording child pornography.
byline: Jérémie Pham-Lê and  Lorraine de Foucher

title: Moldovan President Maia Sandu: 'For us, joining the EU is not just a dream but a survival strategy as a democratic state'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, President Maia Sandu advocates for the European integration of Moldova, Ukraine and the Western Balkans to withstand Russian pressure. She also raises the possibility of unifying her country with Romania to speed up the process.

title: The French love their public libraries. But lack of resources undermines the experience
subhead: France's unique network of 15,500 local cultural spaces, which are open to all, free of charge, has seen visitor numbers steadily increase, leading to certain issues.
